title: Worm SPRG Concept — Character Creation, Standard Start:
excerpt: Players will ‘draft’ their character, getting three random options for four different categories, until they have a prepared starting character. They can check all their options and move between stages, locking in choices until they’re

title: Worm SPRG Concept — Choosing Powers
excerpt:
There are twelve classifications or general classes of parahuman, broad categories of power:

Mover

Relate to enhanced movement

Blaster

Power is a long ranged attack or offense

Shaker

Have area of effect component

Thinker

Has

title: Worm SPRG Concept — Choosing Personality
excerpt: The character’s personality dictates who they get along with, the events that shape their lives, and abstractly, their approach to things. Events are scenarios or key moments in the character’s day to day that arise, and the bulk of the

title: Worm SPRG Concept — Choosing Archetype
excerpt: Archetype shapes the character’s natural abilities and tendencies by emphasizing the things the individual is good at doing and the things they aren’t as good at. There are seven stats; a character will have two stats they favor and one

title: Worm SPRG Concept — Choosing Disposition
excerpt:
Dispositions reflect a character’s overarching goals and approach. They will have three options to start and pick one. This dictates some events, affiliations, and the means of victory.

The most iconic two are ‘hero’ and ‘villain’, but

title: Worm SPRG Concept — Character Creation, Alternate methods…
excerpt:
Method Two

The player chooses from quotations and statements, answering somewhat abstract prompts. The end result is the same, but it’s abstract, more story-driven, and it works backward from method one, using the fragmented quotations

title: Worm SPRG Concept — Sample Character: Aurora Morset
excerpt:
Mercenary

❸ Brawn

❹ Athletics ⮝

❸ Dexterity

❹ Wits ⮝

❷ Social ⮟

❸ Know

❸ Guts

Archetype: Stalker

Personality

Athletics Up - Movement, stamina, evasion up, climb/jump distances up and costs down, better shot at extending movement.

title: Worm SPRG Concept — Powers, Construction & Handling
excerpt: A primary goal in the Worm SRPG is to have powers that feel sufficiently varied. It should not be common for the same power to be seen twice. With this in mind, each power has at least two aspects that are generated independently of one

title: Worm SPRG Concept — Tutorial Fight: A First Encounter
excerpt: The game opens with a simple ‘mugging the dragon’ fight. If there’s material to borrow from in the trigger, we might drop in elements from the trigger event. Otherwise, it’s the character going about their business when they’re mugged or

title: Worm SPRG Concept — The Gameplay Loop: Phases
excerpt:
The gameplay loop is as follows, after character creation and the tutorial:

Event Phase (or Day Phase) - The player will see two events (one if it’s an Escalation event) drawn from a list determined by their personality and background -

title: Worm SPRG Concept — Events
excerpt: Aurora starts the event portion of the day phase. She has a list of events, drawn from a list compiled from her character’s background and personality. 15 common events, 15 events from ‘Assertive’ (one half of her personality), 10 events

title: Worm SPRG Concept — Schedule
excerpt:
The day phase has a more freeform aspect where Aurora can decide what she does with the day when she isn’t in school or at work.

As always, the sidebar on the right allows viewing of current status, switching between tabs to view

title: Worm SPRG Concept — The City
excerpt: The city is generated and involves a set number of tiles - six to twelve, depending on parameters set as the game opens. Each tile has its traits and some special locations, but to start with, Aurora doesn’t necessarily know much about the

title: Worm SPRG Concept — Combat
excerpt:
Aurora runs into gang members.

The scene opens with a short dialogue, getting into the options available, which may differ each time.

Not every scene is a combat, though they will tend to lean in that direction. In this case, if Aurora

title: Worm SPRG Concept — The Course of the Night
excerpt: The major risk/reward interplay in the conflict phase is how far one pushes their patrols. Aurora wraps up one encounter, and she can decide what she does next. She can retire, a bit premature, or she can carry on. Wounds and bruises

title: Worm SPRG Concept — Teams and Relationships
excerpt: There is no strict limit to how many capes can be on a team, but personalities and conflicts can make things difficult, and it’s not unheard of for teams who grow too large to self destruct. In a game sense, this is an interplay of

title: Worm SPRG Concept — Growth and Progression
excerpt: For a more in-depth (and somewhat outdated) look at growth & progression, as well as a look at some example skills, see the SRPG: Growth doc. Bear in mind there’s some redundant information and examples in there.

title: Worm SPRG Concept — Stats
excerpt: Stats start out between 2-4 each, with some options offering a rare 5 in one stat. The default start is to have +1 in two of the seven stats, and -1 in another. Aurora, our example character, has bonuses to Athletics and Wits, and a minus

title: Worm SPRG Concept — Skills
excerpt: Skills break down to active and passive skills. Active skills go on the hotbar (with limited room) while passive ones are an always-on character effect. A good comparison for skills is relics in Slay the Spire or Risk of Rain; the player

title: Worm SPRG Concept — Progression Across Multiple Runs
excerpt: Players accrue ‘shards’ on finishing a run- losing them if their run ends very early (namely if the player is scumming starts instead of trying to use what’s given in the initial draft). Shards are turned in for Cauldron vials, which are

title: Worm board game
excerpt:
Terms & Basic Info

Characters have three dice colors, red, yellow, and blue. Representing power, cunning & brains, respectively. The number of dice vary, and dice are rolled after use, not when used; so a player could easily have their

title: Worm SRPG: Growth
excerpt:
WORM SRPG: GROWTH

Key notes on skills:

Skills shouldn’t be powers, they represent the growth of the person, the little and big things that they do or can do that assist them.

If they interact with powers, they should do so incidentally

title: Worm SRPG: Growth — Broader Growth: Filling out the Sheet
excerpt:
During character creation, in addition to powers, one chooses:

Archetype - the broader things one excels at and what they’re poor at.

Fully fleshed out, boosts two stats and penalizes one (and partially penalizes a third). A character

title: Worm SRPG: Growth — Stats
excerpt:
There are seven major statistics that determine one’s aptitudes and weaknesses. The default for a stat is 3, but tend to range between 2 and 5 for starting characters.

The stats are:

Brawn

Athletics

Dexterity

Wits

Social

Knowledge

title: Worm SRPG: Growth — Raising Stats
excerpt: Stats start between 2-4 and as the character improves and undertakes challenges, they’ll get incremental improvements to these stats. Through training and events, and through taking skills, the character will hone their natural abilities.

title: Worm SRPG: Growth — Diminishing Returns
excerpt: As stats are raised, it gets more difficult to raise them. For example, the low chance of a .1 to .3 improvement from training (which is more typically used to hone skills) will become a .0 to .2 improvement after a set number of

title: Worm SRPG: Growth — Skills
excerpt: Luis starts with one skill (Guarded Stance), which is an added action he can take in combat. All characters start with an active (that is, usable in combat) skill relevant to the first of their two major stats. After his first encounter,

title: Worm SRPG: Growth — Acquisition of Skills
excerpt:
Learning Skills

One of the rewards given for making certain decisions during events, succeeding in confrontations and objectives, are keys to skill acquisition. They represent progress in a given area, and are colored based on the stat

title: Worm SRPG: Growth — Appendix: Luis’ skill options
